summary: 9/10 ACs proven via live recipe (9/9 PASS) + 221/221 jest tests. AC4 (Tab order) UNTESTABLE — recipe does not exercise Tab sequence; native DOM order assumed. No weak/missing rows.

ac_to_node_binding.reason: All AC subflows use ac<N>-<verb>-<obj> node IDs; setup/teardown prefixes correct.

screenshot_evidence.reason: 4 evidence PNGs (min-order-empty, valid-amount, limit-autofocus, enter-submit-redirect) directly corroborate caption claims.

trace_alignment.reason: trace.json shows 9/9 subflows ok=true; passed counts (2,2,3,2,3,4,8,5,14) align with coverage matrix.

forbidden_patterns.reason: No `switch default` skipping ACs; no skip-reason eval_sync; no >500ms wait; no DOM-only proofs for visual AC.

auto_issues.reason: Issue review status `review` — 38× home 'Unknown action Object' errors + 7× SW HttpRequestError AbortError + 7× SW Sentry-not-initialized. Non-gating; pre-existing background noise unrelated to PR.

ac_coverage.reason: AC4 (Tab key sequence) not exercised by recipe. Marked UNTESTABLE with rationale; manual reviewer must Tab-walk before merge.

better_version_guidance: Add a `tab-key-sequence` subflow that programmatically dispatches Tab keydown events and asserts focus moves through size → leverage → (auto-close-TP if shown) → submit; mirror the same for TP/SL modal (TP-price → TP-% → SL-price → SL-%). Even if the underlying mechanism is native DOM order, an explicit assertion catches accidental tabIndex regressions.

Move the canonical `perps/open-order-form` flow into temp/recipes/domains/perps/flows/ on `main` so future review slots do not need to copy it from temp/agentic/. The author's task-local recipe assumes it lives there.

Default `sideLabel` in ac5-valid-amount.json was 'Long' (title-case), but i18n key `perpsOpenLong` resolves to 'Open long $1' (lowercase 'l'). Fixed in this review (changed default to 'long'); upstream the same fix to author's bundle.

Add a metaRPCClientFactory.test.js case that pushes a stream response with `error.message = ''` and asserts the pending request rejects with the safe-message string instead of orphaning the promise. The behavioral fix in this PR has no unit-test guard.
